settings.label: Acceleration Struct Size Padding
settings.description: Increase the required acceleration structure size that is reported to the application by calls to `GetRaytracingAccelerationStructurePrebuildInfo`. This can enable replay in environments with increased acceleration structure size requirements. The value should be specified as a percent of size increase. For example, a value of `5` would increase the reported acceleration structure sizes by `5%`.

settings.label: Allow Application Compiled Pipeline Behavior
settings.description: The default behavior forces VK_PIPELINE_COMPILE_REQUIRED to be returned from Create*Pipelines calls which have VK_PIPELINE_CREATE_FAIL_ON_PIPELINE_COMPILE_REQUIRED_BIT set, and skips dispatching and recording the calls. This forces applications to fallback to recompiling pipelines without caching, the Vulkan calls for which will be captured. Enabling this option causes capture to record the application's calls and implementation's return values unmodified, but the resulting captures are fragile to changes in Vulkan implementations if they use pipeline caching.

settings.label: Capture Dump Android Assets
settings.description: Setting this triggers a dump of all assets into the asset file. Since Android options cannot be set by the layer, dumping is done whenever this option switches between from `false` to `true` or from `true` to `false`. [Deprecated, please use 'capture_dynamic_trigger_dump_assets']

settings.label: Capture Trigger For Android
settings.description: Set during runtime to `true` to start capturing and to `false` to stop. If not set at all then it is disabled (non-trimmed capture). [Deprecated, please use 'capture_dynamic_trigger']

settings.label: Capture File Compression Format
settings.description: Compression format to use with the capture file. Valid values are: LZ4, ZLIB, ZSTD, and NONE.

flags.label: LZ4
flags.description: Fastest compression algorithm that favors performance over compression ratio.

flags.label: ZLIB
flags.description: Widely used compression algorithm with better compression ratio than LZ4 but worse performance.

flags.label: ZSTD
flags.description: Newer compression algorithm with better compression ratio and performance than ZLIB, though not as fast as LZ4.

settings.label: Capture Environment
settings.description: Comma delimited list of environment variables to store in the capture file. These can optionally be restored during replay to their capture-time values with the `gfxrecon-replay-renamed.py` utility.

settings.label: Capture Specific Draw Calls
settings.description: Specify one index or a range indices drawcalls(include dispatch) based on a ExecuteCommandList index and a CommandList index to capture. The index is 0-based. The args are one submit index, one command index, one or a range indices of draw calls, one or a range indices of bundle draw calls(option), like `0,0,0` or `0,0,0-2` or `0,0,0-2,0`. The forth arg is an option for bundle case. If the the 3rd arg is a bundle commandlist, but it doesn't set the 4th arg, it will set 0 as default. If not defined, all drawcalls are captured.

settings.label: Capture File Name
settings.description: Path to use when creating the capture file. Supports variable patterns for dynamic file paths, such as `${AppName}` (the application or executable name).

settings.label: Capture File Flush After Write
settings.description: Flush output stream after each packet is written to the capture file.

settings.label: Capture File Name with Timestamp
settings.description: Add a timestamp (yyyymmddThhmmss) postfix to the capture file name.

settings.label: Capture Specific Frames
settings.description: Specify one or more comma-separated frame ranges to capture. Each range will be written to its own file. A frame range can be specified as a single value, to specify a single frame to capture, or as two hyphenated values, to specify the first and last frame to capture. Frame ranges should be specified in ascending order and cannot overlap. Note that frame numbering is 1-based (i.e. the first frame is frame 1). Example: 200,301-305 will create two capture files, one containing a single frame and one containing five frames. If not defined, all frames are captured.

settings.label: Capture IID_IUnknown Wrapping
settings.description: If GFXRECON_CAPTURE_IUNKNOWN_WRAPPING is enabled detect when applications call QueryInterface(IID_IUnknown) and switch IID_IUnknown to original IID, so that GFXR returns original interface wrapper.

settings.label: Capture Specific Process Name
settings.description: The name of the process to capture content from, all other processes will be ignored.  All valid API processes are captured otherwise.

settings.label: Capture Specific GPU Queue Submits
settings.description: Specify one or more comma-separated GPU queue submit call ranges to capture.  Queue submit calls are `vkQueueSubmit` for Vulkan and `ID3D12CommandQueue::ExecuteCommandLists` for DX12. Queue submit ranges work as described for `capture_frames` but on GPU queue submit calls instead of frames. The index is 0-based. Default is all queue submits are captured.

settings.label: Capture Restrictions
settings.description: Restrictions That Can Be Placed on Capture

settings.label: Hot Keys
settings.description: Hot Keys for Dynamic Capture

settings.label: Hotkey Capture Trigger
settings.description: Specify a hotkey (any one of F1-F12, TAB, CONTROL) that will be used to start/stop capture. Example: F3 will set the capture trigger to F3 hotkey. One capture file will be generated for each pair of start/stop hotkey presses.   This, in effect, toggles `capture_dynamic_trigger`.

settings.label: Dynamic Capture Trigger
settings.description: Set during runtime to `true` to start capturing and to `false` to stop. If not set at all then it is disabled (non-trimmed capture). [Replaces 'capture_android_trigger']

settings.label: Dynamic Capture Trigger Dump Assets
settings.description: Setting this triggers a dump of all assets into the asset file. Dumping is done whenever this option switches between from `false` to `true` or from `true` to `false`. [Replaces 'capture_android_dump_assets']

settings.label: Hotkey Capture Frame Limit
settings.description: Specify a limit on the number of frames to be captured via hotkey. Example: `1` will capture exactly one frame when the trigger key is pressed. If not defined, then there is no limit

settings.label: Use Asset File
settings.description: When set to `true` assets (images, buffers and descriptors) will be stored separately into an asset file instead of the capture file.

settings.label: Debug Device Lost
settings.description: Enables automatic injection of breadcrumbs into command buffers and page fault reporting. Used to debug device removed problems.

settings.label: Enable Debug Layer
settings.description: Enable the Direct3D debug layer for Direct3D 12 application captures.

settings.label: Disable DXR Support
settings.description: Override the result of `CheckFeatureSupport` to report the `RaytracingTier` as `D3D12_RAYTRACING_TIER_NOT_SUPPORTED`.

settings.label: Disable MetaCommmand Support
settings.description: Override the result of `CheckFeatureSupport` to report the D3D12_FEATURE_QUERY_META_COMMAND is invalid. Some games might use DirectStorage to improve performance, GFXR could not fully support the feature. By using this option, could bypass the feature and force game to use regular I/O to read from harddisk.

settings.label: Force Command Serialization
settings.description: Sets exclusive locks (unique_lock) for every ApiCall. It can avoid external multi-thread to cause captured issue.

settings.label: Force FIFO Present Mode
settings.description: When the `force_fifo_present_mode` is enabled, force all present modes in vkGetPhysicalDeviceSurfacePresentModesKHR to VK_PRESENT_MODE_FIFO_KHR, app present mode is set in vkCreateSwapchain to VK_PRESENT_MODE_FIFO_KHR. Otherwise the original present mode will be used.

settings.label: Ignore vkFrameBoundaryANDROID
settings.description: Ignore any calls to vkFrameBoundaryANDROID when determining frame boundaries.

settings.label: Log
settings.description: Control log messages.

settings.label: Log Apply Additional Indentation
settings.description: Apply additional indentation formatting to log messages.

settings.label: Log Break on Error
settings.description: Trigger a debug break when logging an error.

settings.label: Log Name and Line Number
settings.description: Include name and line number from the file responsible for the log message.

settings.label: Log Errors to StdError
settings.description: Log any errors that occur to stderr.

settings.label: Log File
settings.description: When set, log messages will be written to a file at the specified location.

settings.label: Log File Overwrite
settings.description: Specifies that log file initialization should overwrite an existing file when `true`, or append to an existing file when `false`.

settings.label: Log File Flush After Write
settings.description: Flush the log file to disk after each write when true.

settings.label: Log File Keep Open
settings.description: Keep the log file open between log messages when true, or close and reopen the log file for each message when false.

settings.label: Log Level
settings.description: Specify the highest level message to log.  The specified level and all levels listed after it will be enabled for logging.  For example, choosing the `warning` level will also enable the `error` and `fatal` levels.

settings.label: Log Output to Console / stdout
settings.description: Log messages will be written to stdout.

settings.label: Log Output to Debug Console
settings.description: Log messages will be written to the Debug Console with OutputDebugStringA

settings.label: Log Timestamps
settings.description: Output a timestamp in front of each log message.

settings.label: Memory Tracking Mode
settings.description: Specifies the memory tracking mode to use for detecting modifications to mapped Vulkan memory objects. Available options are: page_guard, assisted, and unassisted.

flags.label: page_guard
flags.description: Tracks modifications to individual memory pages, which are written to the capture file on calls to vkFlushMappedMemoryRanges, vkUnmapMemory, and vkQueueSubmit. Tracking modifications requires allocating shadow memory for all mapped memory.

flags.label: assisted
flags.description: Expects the application to call vkFlushMappedMemoryRanges after memory is modified; the memory ranges specified to the vkFlushMappedMemoryRanges call will be written to the capture file during the call.

flags.label: unassisted
flags.description: Writes the full content of mapped memory to the capture file on calls to vkUnmapMemory and vkQueueSubmit. It is very inefficient and may be unusable with real-world applications that map large amounts of memory.

settings.label: Page Guard Align Buffer Sizes
settings.description: When the `page_guard` memory tracking mode is enabled, this option overrides the Vulkan API calls that report buffer memory properties to report that buffer sizes and alignments must be a multiple of the system page size. This option is intended to be used with applications that perform CPU writes and GPU writes/copies to different buffers that are bound to the same page of mapped memory, which may result in data being lost when copying pages from the page_guard shadow allocation to the real allocation. This data loss can result in visible corruption during capture. Forcing buffer sizes and alignments to a multiple of the system page size prevents multiple buffers from being bound to the same page, avoiding data loss from simultaneous CPU writes to the shadow allocation and GPU writes to the real allocation for different buffers bound to the same page.

settings.label: Page Guard Copy on Map
settings.description: When the `page_guard` memory tracking mode is enabled, copies the content of the mapped memory to the shadow memory immediately after the memory is mapped.

settings.label: Page Guard External Memory
settings.description: When the `page_guard` memory tracking mode is enabled, use the VK_EXT_external_memory_host extension to eliminate the need for shadow memory allocations. For each memory allocation from a host visible memory type, the capture layer will create an allocation from system memory, which it can monitor for write access, and provide that allocation to vkAllocateMemory as external memory.

settings.label: Page Guard Persistent Memory
settings.description: When the `page_guard` memory tracking mode is enabled, this option changes the way that the shadow memory used to detect modifications to mapped memory is allocated. The default behavior is to allocate and copy the mapped memory range on map and free the allocation on unmap. When this option is enabled, an allocation with a size equal to that of the object being mapped is made once on the first map and is not freed until the object is destroyed. This option is intended to be used with applications that frequently map and unmap large memory ranges, to avoid frequent allocation and copy operations that can have a negative impact on performance. This option is ignored when `page_guard_external_memory` is enabled.

settings.label: Page Guard Separate Read Tracking
settings.description: When the `page_guard` memory tracking mode is enabled, copies the content of pages accessed for read from mapped memory to shadow memory on each read. Can overwrite unprocessed shadow memory content when an application is reading from and writing to the same page.

settings.label: Page Guard Signal Handler Watcher
settings.description: When the `page_guard` memory tracking mode is enabled, setting this to `true` will spawn a thread which will will periodically reinstall the `SIGSEGV` handler if it has been replaced by the application being traced.

settings.label: Page Guard Signal Handler Watcher Max Restores
settings.description: Sets the number of times the watcher will attempt to restore the signal handler. Setting it to a negative will make the watcher thread run indefinitely.

settings.label: Page Guard Track AHB Memory
settings.description: When the `page_guard` memory tracking mode is enabled, eliminate the need for shadow memory allocations for Android Hardware Buffers.

settings.label: Page Guard Unblock SIGSEGV
settings.description: When the `page_guard` memory tracking mode is enabled and in the case that SIGSEGV has been marked as blocked in thread's signal mask, setting this to `true` will forcibly re-enable the signal in the thread's signal mask.

settings.label: Queue Zero Only
settings.description: Forces to using only QueueFamilyIndex: 0 and queueCount: 1 on capturing to avoid replay error for unavailable VkQueue.

settings.label: Quit after capturing frame ranges
settings.description: Specify one or more comma-separated frame ranges to capture. Each range will be written to its own file. A frame range can be specified as a single value, to specify a single frame to capture, or as two hyphenated values, to specify the first and last frame to capture. Frame ranges should be specified in ascending order and cannot overlap. Note that frame numbering is 1-based (i.e. the first frame is frame 1). Example: 200,301-305 will create two capture files, one containing a single frame and one containing five frames. If not defined, all frames are captured.

settings.label: Specify GPU Descriptor Handle RV Annotation
settings.description: By default, the 2-byte identifier values are hard-coded. This option forces a specific identifier value to be used for annotating GPU descriptors. The value should be specified as a 2-byte hexadecimal string, e.g., `0xAB12` or `ab12`.

settings.label: Enable Experimental RV Search
settings.description: Experimental feature to help enable replay of certain DXR/ExecuteIndirect workloads. RV annotation is a capture mode which attempts to detect when applications write Resource Values (RVs) to memory. Conceptually, RVs represent different types of GPU pointers that applications write as resource data. This capture mode writes GFXR-specific identifier values into unoccupied bits of application-facing RVs and then searches for the identifier values when the application performs a memory write. This allows GFXR to better track RV locations and eventually produce an RV-optimized capture file that may be replayed. Enabling this feature introduces performance overhead during capture, and may result in unstable capture and/or replay.

settings.label: Specify GPU VA RV Annotation
settings.description: By default, the 2-byte identifier values are hard-coded. This option forces a specific identifier value to be used for annotating GPUVAs. The value should be specified as a 2-byte hexadecimal string, e.g., `0xAB12` or `ab12`.

settings.label: Use Random RV Annotation
settings.description: By default, the 2-byte identifier values are hard-coded. This option generates random identifier values used for annotating GPUVAs and GPU descriptors. Use this if capture-time crashes are observed.

settings.label: Screenshot
settings.description: Screenshot Control

settings.label: Screenshot Directory
settings.description: Directory used where screenshots are saved to.  Default is the current working directory.

settings.label: Screenshot Format
settings.description: Image file format to use when saving screenshots.

settings.label: Screenshot Specific Frames
settings.description: Specify one or more comma-separated frame ranges to screenshot. Each range will be written to its own file. A frame range can be specified as a single value, to specify a single frame to capture, or as two hyphenated values, to specify the first and last frame to capture. Frame ranges should be specified in ascending order and cannot overlap. Note that frame numbering is 1-based (i.e. the first frame is frame 1).

settings.label: Screenshot Specific Frames
settings.description: Generate screenshots for the specified frames. Target frames are specified as a comma separated list of frame ranges.  A frame range can be specified as a single value, to specify a single frame, or as two hyphenated values, to specify the first and last frames to process.  Frame ranges should be specified in ascending order and cannot overlap.  Note that frame numbering is 1-based (i.e. the first frame is frame 1). Example: 200,301-305 will generate six screenshots.

settings.label: Screenshot Interval
settings.description: Sets the number between every screenshot taken.  1 means every frame is screenshot, 2 means every other, etc. Example: If screenshot range is 10-15 and interval is 2, screenshot will be generated for frames 10, 12 and 14.

settings.label: Skip Threads With Invalid Data
settings.description: When a thread is encountered which contains data that is unexpected, skip the data and mark the thread as skippable.  This is important especially in OpenXR where other API commands (such as Vulkan) may be generated inside of the OpenXR commands, but may still be referenced in some fashion outside of the OpenXR commands.  This results in issues during replay.  So, this option prevents those commands, and the threads containing those commands from being recorded to the capture file.

settings.label: Create vkPipelineCache for All Pipelines
settings.description: If set, allows gfxreconstruct to create new vkPipelineCache objects when it encounters a pipeline created without cache. This option can be used in coordination with `--save-pipeline-cache` and `--load-pipeline-cache`.

settings.label: Allowed Debug Messages
settings.description: Identify messages to allow D3D12 debugging to output

settings.label: Percent Memory Allowed
settings.description: Max amount of memory consumption while loading a trimmed capture file. Acceptable values range from 0 to 100. 0 means no batching at all 100 means use all available system and GPU memory

settings.label: Capture the Replaying File
settings.description: Capture the replaying GFXR file. Capture uses the same log options as replay. All other capture option behavior and usage is the same as when capturing with the GFXR layer. The capture functionality is included in the `gfxrecon-replay` executable -- no GFXR capture layer is added to the Vulkan layer chain.

settings.label: Set CPU Core Usage
settings.description: Set of CPU cores used by the replayer. `binary-mask` is a succession of '0' and '1' read from left to right that specifies used/unused cores. For example '10010' activates the first and fourth cores and deactivate all other cores. If the option is not set, all cores can be used. If the option is set only for some cores, the other cores are not used.

settings.label: Create Dummy Allocations
settings.description: Enable creation of dummy heaps and resources for replay validation.

settings.label: Debug Messenger Level
settings.description: Specify highest debug messenger severity level. Options are: debug, info, warning, and error.

settings.label: Denied Debug Messages
settings.description: Identify messages to deny D3D12 debugging to output

settings.label: De-Duplicate Device
settings.description: If set, at most one VkDevice will be created for each VkPhysicalDevice for RenderDoc and DXVK case.

settings.label: Discard Cached PSOs
settings.description: Discard cached PSOs [Deprecated, please use '--use-cached-psos'].

settings.label: Enable Dump Resources
settings.description: Dump out resources during replay

argument-description-array.description: The capture file will be examined, and <submit-index,command-index,draw-call-index> will be converted to <arg> as used in `--dump-resources` <arg> below. The converted args will be used as the args for dump resources.
Extract `--dump-resources` block indices args from the specified json file. The format for the json file is documented in detail in the gfxreconstruct documentation.

settings.label: Dump Resource Before Draw Calls
settings.description: In addition to dumping GPU resources after the draw calls specified by the `--dump-resources` argument, also dump resources before the draw calls.

settings.label: Dump Resources Compression
settings.description: Compress files that are dumped as binary.

flags.label: LZ4
flags.description: Fastest compression algorithm that favors performance over compression ratio.

flags.label: ZLIB
flags.description: Widely used compression algorithm with better compression ratio than LZ4 but worse performance.

flags.label: ZSTD
flags.description: Newer compression algorithm with better compression ratio and performance than ZLIB, though not as fast as LZ4.

settings.label: Dump Resources Directory
settings.description: Directory to write dump resources output files. Default is the current working directory.

settings.label: Dump All Image Levels and Layers
settings.description: Dump all available mip levels and layers when dumping images.

settings.label: Dump All Input Buffers Used to Build Acceleration Structs
settings.description: Dump all input buffers used in vkCmdBuildAccelerationStructures. This includes vertex, index, transformation matrix, AABB and instance buffers. Default is off.

settings.label: Dump Color Attachments at Index
settings.description: Configures which color attachment to dump when dumping draw calls.  Default is all attachments.

settings.label: Dump the Depth Attachment of Draw Calls
settings.description: Configures whether to dump the depth attachment of draw calls. Default is false.

settings.label: Dump Raw Images
settings.description: Dump the images in raw format (without conversion), otherwise they will be transformed into a VK_FORMAT_B8G8R8A8_* format, more suitable for dumping in an image file.

settings.label: Dump Image Alpha Separately
settings.description: Dump the image alpha as a separate file from the main image.

settings.label: Dump Unused Vertex Bindings
settings.description: Dump a vertex binding even if no vertex attributes references it.

settings.label: Dump Vertex/Index Buffers
settings.description: Enable dumping of vertex and index buffers while dumping draw call resources. Default is disabled.

settings.label: Dump Resources Image Format
settings.description: Image file format to use when dumping image resources. Available formats are: bmp, png.

settings.label: Enable Dumped Command JSON
settings.description: Enable storing a json output file for each dumped command.  Overrides default behavior which is generating one output json file that contains the information for all dumped commands.

settings.label: Dump Only Modifiable Resources
settings.description: Only dump resources that are in a modifiable state set by D3D12 ResourceBarrier.

settings.label: Dump Resources Images Scale
settings.description: Scale images generated by dump resources by the given scale factor. The scale factor must be a floating point number greater than 0. Values greater than 10 are capped at 10. Default value is 1.0.

settings.label: D3D12 Inject Debug Markers
settings.description: Label each API calls as block index of the trace Radeon GPU Detective could dump the label for debugging.

settings.label: D3D12 Override Object Names
settings.description: Generate unique names for all ID3D12Objects and assign each object the generated name.  This is intended to assist in replay debugging.

settings.label: D3D12 Two Pass Replay
settings.description: Enable D3D12 Two Pass Replay.

settings.label: Flush Only Inside Measurement Range
settings.description: If this is specified the replayer will flush and wait for all current GPU work to finish at the end of each frame inside the measurement range.

settings.label: Range of Frames to Flush/Wait
settings.description: If this is specified the replayer will flush and wait for all current GPU work to finish at the start and end of the measurement range.

settings.label: Force Windowed
settings.description: Setup windowed and override resolution.

settings.label: Force Window Origin
settings.description: Setup windowed origin.

settings.label: Use GPU Index
settings.description: Use the specified device for replay, where index is the zero-based index to the array of physical devices returned by vkEnumeratePhysicalDevices or IDXGIFactory1::EnumAdapters1. Replay may fail if the specified device is not compatible with the original capture devices.

settings.label: Use GPU Group Index
settings.description: Use the specified device group for replay, where index is the zero-based index to the array of physical device group returned by vkEnumeratePhysicalDeviceGroups.  Replay may fail if the specified device group is not compatible with the original capture device group.

settings.label: Print Usage
settings.description: Print usage information and exit

settings.label: Input Capture File
settings.description: The name of the capture file to use for processing.  This overrides the non-tagged input filename that used to appear after all optional arguments

settings.label: Load Pipeline Cache
settings.description: If set, loads data created by the `--save-pipeline-cache` option in <cache-file> and uses it to create the pipelines instead of the pipeline caches saved at capture time.

settings.label: Log messages with OutputDebugStringA.
settings.description: Log messages with OutputDebugStringA.

settings.label: Write Measurements To File
settings.description: Write measurements to a file at the specified path. Default is: '/sdcard/gfxrecon-measurements.json' on Android and'./gfxrecon-measurements.json' on desktop.

settings.label: Write Measurements To File
settings.description: Custom framerange to measure FPS for. This range will include the start frame but not the end frame. The measurement frame range defaults to all frames except the loading frame but can be configured for any range. If the end frame is past the last frame in the trace it will be clamped to the frame after the last (so in that case the results would include the last frame).

settings.label: Memory Translation
settings.description: Enable memory translation for replay on GPUs with memory types that are not compatible with the capture GPU's memory types

flags.label: REMAP
flags.description: Attempt to map capture memory types to compatible replay memory types, without altering memory allocation behavior.

flags.label: REALIGN
flags.description: Adjust memory allocation sizes and resource binding offsets based on replay memory properties.

flags.label: REBIND
flags.description: Change memory allocation behavior based on resource usage and replay memory properties.  Resources may be bound to different allocations with different offsets.  Uses VMA to manage allocations and suballocations.

settings.label: Disable Windows Debug Pop-up
settings.description: Disable the 'Abort, Retry, Ignore' message box displayed when abort() is called (Windows debug only).

settings.label: Insert Offscreen Frame Identifiers
settings.description: Should only be used with offscreen swapchain. Activates the extension VK_EXT_frame_boundary (always supported if trimming, checks for driver support otherwise) and inserts command buffer submission with VkFrameBoundaryEXT where vkQueuePresentKHR was called in the original capture.  This allows preserving frames when capturing a replay that uses offscreen swapchain.

settings.label: Omit NULL AHBs
settings.description: Omit calls that use a NULL AHardwareBuffer

settings.label: Omit Using Pipeline Cache
settings.description: Omit pipeline cache data from calls to vkCreatePipelineCache and skip calls to vkGetPipelineCacheData

settings.label: Pause At Frame
settings.description: Pause after replay frame number <frame>

settings.label: Pause After First Frame
settings.description: Pause after replaying the first frame (same as `--pause-frame 1`).

settings.label: Print All Block Info
settings.description: Print all block information.

settings.label: Print Block Info
settings.description: Print block information between block index1 and block index2.

settings.label: Pipeline Creation Jobs
settings.description: Specify the number of asynchronous pipeline-creation jobs as integer. If <num_jobs> is negative it will be added to the number of cpu-cores Default: 0 (do not use asynchronous operations).

settings.label: Range of Frames To Pre-Load
settings.description: Preload the provided measurement range of frames.

settings.label: Present Mode
settings.description: Override the VkPresentModeKHR of the swapchain value during replay

flags.description: Use whatever present mode the original application used.

settings.label: Quit Replay After Frame
settings.description: Quit replay after frame <frame> has completed.

settings.label: Quit After Measurement Range
settings.description: If this is specified the replayer will abort when it reaches the <end_frame> specified in the `--measurement-frame-range` argument.

settings.label: Remove Unsupported Extensions/Features
settings.description: Remove unsupported extensions and features from instance and device creation parameters.

settings.label: Replace Shaders
settings.description: Replace the shader code in each CreateShaderModule with the contents of the file <dir>/sh<handle_id> if found, where <handle_id> is the handle id of the CreateShaderModule call. See gfxrecon-extract.

settings.label: Save Pipeline Cache
settings.description: If set, produces pipeline caches at replay time instead of using the one saved at capture time and save those caches in <cache-file>

settings.label: Screenshot All Frames
settings.description: Generate screenshots for all frames.  When this option is specified, other screenshot frame-related options are ignored.

settings.label: Ignore Frames Using vkFrameBoundANDROID
settings.description: If set, frames switched with vkFrameBoundANDROID will be ignored from the screenshot handler.

settings.label: Screenshot Prefix
settings.description: Prefix to apply to the screenshot file name.  Default is 'screenshot', producing file names similar to 'screenshot_frame_8049.bmp'

settings.label: Screenshot Scale
settings.description: Specify a decimal factor which will determine screenshot sizes. The factor will be multiplied with the swapchain images dimension to determine the screenshot dimensions. Default value is 1.0.

settings.label: Screenshot Size
settings.description: Specify desired screenshot dimensions. Leaving this unspecified screenshots will use the swapchain images dimensions. If `--screenshot-scale` is also specified then this option is ignored.

settings.label: Skip Failed Allocations
settings.description: Skip vkAllocateMemory, vkAllocateCommandBuffers, and vkAllocateDescriptorSets calls that failed during capture.

settings.label: Skip Get Fence Ranges
settings.description: Frame ranges where `--sgfs` applies. The format is: <frame-start-1>-<frame-end-1>[,<frame-start-1>-<frame-end-1>]*

settings.label: Skip Get Fence Status
settings.description: Specify behaviour to skip calls to vkWaitForFences and vkGetFenceStatus. <status> = 0: Don't skip, <status> = 1: Skip unsuccessful calls, <status> = 2: Always Skip. If no skip frame range is specified (through `--sgfr`), the status applies to all frames. Skip vkGetFenceStatus calls that return a provided status

settings.label: Restrict to Surface Index
settings.description: Restrict rendering to the Nth surface object created. Used with captures that include multiple surfaces.  Default is -1 (render to all surfaces).

settings.label: Swapchain Type
settings.description: Choose a swapchain mode to replay.

flags.label: CAPTURED
flags.description: Use the swapchain indices stored in the capture directly on the swapchain setup for replay.

flags.label: OFFSCREEN
flags.description: Disable creating swapchains, surfaces and windows. To see rendering, add the --screenshots option.

flags.label: VIRTUAL
flags.description: Virtual Swapchain of images which match the swapchain in effect at capture time and which are copied to the underlying swapchain of the implementation being replayed on (Default).

settings.label: Sync After Queue Submits
settings.description: Synchronize after each queue submission with vkQueueWaitIdle.

settings.label: Permit Using Cached PSOs
settings.description: Permit using cached PSOs when creating graphics or compute pipelines.  Using cached PSOs may reduce PSO creation time but may result in replay errors.

settings.label: Use Captured Swapchain Indicies
settings.description: Same as `--swapchain captured`.  Ignored if the `--swapchain` option is used.

settings.label: Fallback Swapchain Colorspace For Replay Device
settings.description: Swap the swapchain color space if unsupported by replay device. Check if color space is not supported by replay device and fallback to VK_COLOR_SPACE_SRGB_NONLINEAR_KHR.

settings.label: Enable Validation
settings.description: Enable the Khronos Vulkan validation layer when replaying a Vulkan capture or the Direct3D debug layer when replaying a Direct3D 12 capture.

settings.label: Print Version
settings.description: Print version information and exit.

settings.label: Skip BLTs in Virtual Swapchain
settings.description: If using Virtual Swapchain, skip the actual BLTs to the actual HW surface to gain performance during replay.

settings.label: Wait Before Presenting
settings.description: Force wait on completion of queue operations for all queues before calling Present. This is needed for accurate acquisition of instrumentation data on some platforms.

settings.label: Force WSI
settings.description: Force replay to use the specified wsi platform.

flags.label: AUTO
flags.description: Automatically attempt to determine WSI used based on platform (Default).

flags.label: WIN32
flags.description: Force WIN32 WSI type WSI (Valid on Windows).

flags.label: XLIB
flags.description: Force using XLIB WSI (Valid on Linux).

flags.label: XCB
flags.description: Force using XCB WSI (Valid on Linux).

flags.label: WAYLAND
flags.description: Force using Wayland WSI (Valid on Linux).
